title: impatiens lutea
intro: Impatiens lutea, also known as the yellow jewelweed, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the balsam family. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, such as insect bites and minor burns, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Impatiens lutea include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight against fungal and bacterial infections, and provide relief from itching and irritation. Herbal preparations of Impatiens lutea can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to promote overall well-being.
